rationale: The cited paper appears in a single grouped parenthetical citation used to support a broad background claim that larger choice sets can sometimes harm decision quality. In the citing paper, this serves as general motivation for why FI-RUM welfare monotonicity may be behaviorally unrealistic, but the cited study is not discussed individually. There is no direct reuse of its constructs, design, or findings in model development, estimation, or interpretation, so the role is a passing literature reference rather than a specific building block.
